leadText: Leaf Blade (Japanese: リーフブレード Leaf Blade) is a damage-dealing Grass-type move introduced in Generation III. Prior to Generation IV, it was the signature move of Grovyle and Sceptile. It was TR50 in Pokémon Sword and Shield.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation III 1.2 Generation IV onwards 1.3 Generation VIII 1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.4 Generation IX 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation III [ edit | edit source ] Leaf Blade inflicts damage and has an increased critical hit ratio. It has a base power of 70 in this generation. Generation IV onwards [ edit | edit source ] Leaf Blade's base power increased from 70 to 90. If used by a Pokémon with Sharpness , its power is increased by 50%. Leaf Blade can also be used as part of a Contest Spectacular combination , with the user gaining extra three appeal points if the move Focus Energy was used in the prior turn. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Leaf Blade's PP has been reduced to 10, and its power has been lowered to 85. sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description R S E Colo XD Slashes with a sharp leaf. High critical-hit ratio. FR LG D P Pt HG SS PBR The foe is slashed with a sharp leaf. It has a high critical-hit ratio. B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP The user handles a sharp leaf like a sword and attacks by cutting its target. Critical hits land more easily. LA The user handles a sharp leaf like a sword and attacks by cutting its target. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit. S V The user handles a sharp leaf like a sword and cuts the target to inflict damage. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit. ZA The user attacks by using a leaf like a sword to slice targets. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

The target is slashed with a sharp leaf. It has a high critical-hit ratio. MD GtI S MD MD RT DX It damages an enemy. It has a high critical-hit rate.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] In the Brazilian Portuguese dub of Pokémon the Series: Ruby and Sapphire , from Clamperl of Wisdom to From Cradle to Save , Leaf Blade is referred to as either Folha Navalha or some slight variation of it, a name that had been present in the Brazilian dub since Enter the Dragonite as the national name for Razor Leaf . Interestingly, Leaf Blade is referred to as Lâmina de Folha , its most fitting, and now official, name, from its animated series debut to forty-seven episodes later . It is currently unknown why this name would disappear from the dub until Odd Pokémon Out! .
